content: Hey, I'm feeling a bit stressed about my part-time MBA program. I'm trying to balance school with my work as a Podcast Host, and it's been a challenge.
I can imagine! Balancing work and school can be tough. What's your podcast about, and how do you find time to work on it while pursuing your MBA?

My podcast is about true crime, and I love researching and interviewing guests. It's actually been a great way for me to relax and express myself creatively. I've been trying to fit in podcast-related tasks during my lunch breaks or after work.
That sounds like a fascinating topic! Have you always been interested in true crime, or is this a relatively new passion of yours?

I've always been interested in true crime, but I think what really sparked my interest was a documentary I watched about the Ted Bundy case. After that, I was hooked and started reading everything I could find on the subject.

That's really interesting. It's great that you've found a topic that you're so passionate about. How do you balance your work and school responsibilities with your personal life, and do you have any hobbies or activities that help you relax?

I try to prioritize my time and make sure I'm getting enough rest and exercise. I love hiking and playing tennis, and I've also been getting into calligraphy lately. It's been a great way for me to unwind and express myself creatively.
That sounds like a great way to relax! Calligraphy is such a unique and beautiful art form. Have you thought about incorporating your calligraphy skills into your podcast or MBA program in some way?

That's a great idea! I might consider using my calligraphy skills to create some visual elements for my podcast or even design some marketing materials for my MBA program. I've also been thinking about starting a new podcast series on a specific true crime case, maybe something that happened in Salt Lake City, where I live.

That sounds like a great idea! Salt Lake City has a rich history, and I'm sure you could find some fascinating cases to explore. Have you thought about reaching out to local experts or law enforcement officials to get some insights and interviews for your podcast?

I've actually been in touch with a local detective who's been a great source of information and guidance. I'm excited to start working on this new series and sharing it with my listeners. My friend, Emily, has also been helping me with some research and editing tasks.
That's great! It's always helpful to have a supportive team behind you. What's the timeline looking like for your MBA program, and do you have any plans for after you graduate?
